历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for science (including physics category) was approximately 7523 → 10084 → 8797 → 11329 → 8714, showing an overall fluctuating and backward trend; the 2025 baseline is about 8714, a significant forward shift of about 2615 places compared to 2024, indicating a rebound in application popularity that year; the provincial science enrollment plan was 67 in 2021, 75 in 2022, 74 in 2023, 80 in 2024, and 82 in 2025 (physics category), with a gradual small expansion; the 2026 plan is 82, unchanged from 2025, with no additional expansion effect.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: A 211 Project and Double First-Class university; ranked approximately 49th in the 2026 Soft Science Main Ranking (up 3 places from 52nd in 2025), with a stable and slightly rising ranking, slightly enhancing its appeal to candidates; Transportation Engineering is A+ (top in the country), Civil Engineering is A, directly corresponding to the university's enrollment majors such as Transportation and Civil Engineering, with flagship majors strongly attracting high-scoring candidates; the 2023 reputation evaluation is neutral/mixed, with no significant negative events; Douyin popularity ranks 94th overall (average evaluation), with moderate short video platform exposure, showing no clear signal of increased application attention.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Electronic Information/Communication, Electrical Engineering and Automation, Computer Science, etc., are highly popular and recommended, aligning with the university's advantageous engineering fields; Civil Engineering and Architecture are divided and watched, with relatively cold popularity. The total number of candidates in the province for the 2026 college entrance examination is about 154,000, a significant increase of about 32.4% compared to 2025 (history category + physics category totaling 116,360), sharply intensifying competition; the university is located in Chengdu, Sichuan, in the southwest region, with no obvious regional preference bonus for Inner Mongolia candidates; local enrollment accounts for about 1.3%–1.5%, which is normal for out-of-province enrollment.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the rebound trend of a significant forward shift in 2025 rankings, the unchanged 2026 enrollment plan, a notable increase in the total number of candidates in the province (about 32.4%), and a slight rise in the Soft Science ranking, it is expected that competition in science in 2026 will further intensify compared to 2025, and the minimum admission ranking will continue to move forward (decrease in number). The predicted minimum ranking is about 8564, with a range of 8064–9064.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: If your ranking is within the predicted range (8064–9064), it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors as the main choice, sprint for Tier 1 flagship majors, and use Tier 3 majors as safety options. If your ranking is significantly better than the upper limit of the range (<8064), you can focus on sprinting for A+ flagship majors such as Transportation and Electrical Engineering; if your ranking is close to the lower limit of the range (>9064), you should mainly rely on Tier 3 majors as safety options, while also considering Tier 2 medium majors.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for liberal arts (including history category) was approximately 1004 → 2476 → 2482 → 2869 → 1353. From 2022 to 2024, the ranking continuously moved backward, but in 2025 it moved significantly forward by about 1516 places, showing drastic fluctuations. The baseline for 2025 is approximately 1353. The enrollment plan for liberal arts in this province was 17 in 2021, 10 each from 2022 to 2024, and 8 in 2025 (history category), showing a decreasing trend. The 2026 plan is 9, a slight increase of 1 (+12.5%) compared to 2025, slightly easing competition. It is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university; its Soft Science main ranking in 2026 is approximately 49th, with a stable and slightly rising position.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: The university's reputation evaluation is neutral/mixed, with no significant negative public opinion. Its popularity on Douyin is average, with low exposure for liberal arts-related majors. Transportation Engineering (A+) and Civil Engineering (A) are both science and engineering disciplines, which have no direct disciplinary evaluation correspondence with the liberal arts enrollment majors (Chinese Language and Literature, Foreign Languages and Literature, Ideological and Political Education, Public Administration). Liberal arts majors lack A-level disciplinary support.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The total number of candidates for the 2026 college entrance examination in this province is approximately 154,000, a significant increase of about 32.4% compared to 2025. The estimated number of history category candidates is about 43,200 (an increase of about 32.3% from 32,644 in 2025), intensifying competition. The university is located in Chengdu, Sichuan, with no obvious regional preference for liberal arts candidates from Inner Mongolia. The small number of liberal arts enrollments (8–9) makes ranking fluctuations easily affected by the application behavior of a few high-scoring candidates.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the rebound trend of a significant forward shift in 2025 rankings, the offsetting effects of a slight enrollment expansion in 2026 (+1 person) and a substantial increase in total candidates (about 32.3%), as well as the small number of liberal arts enrollments and large ranking fluctuations, it is expected that the minimum admission ranking for liberal arts in 2026 will slightly move forward (decrease in number) from the 2025 baseline. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 1425, with a range of 1211–1639.
